steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a "function of underlined text" question. The underlined phrase "(film containing scaled-down reproductions of documents)" appears in parentheses immediately after the word "microfilm" — a classic signal that the parenthetical is defining the preceding term.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It gives information about a famous person.': The parenthetical describes film technology, not Frederick Douglass or any person. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It explains the meaning of a word.': The phrase restates "microfilm" in plain language ("film containing scaled-down reproductions of documents"), functioning as an inline definition. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It describes a debate among historians.': No debate appears anywhere in the underlined portion or the sentence containing it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It summarizes an unexpected finding.': A parenthetical definition cannot summarize a finding; it merely clarifies a term. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
